title: Hybrid 3 Agent Workflow — Google Antigravity + Claude Code + Gemini
summary: A multi-agent coding loop that chains Claude Code (primary builder), Google Antigravity (web/external search layer), and Gemini (search/context expansion) to extend usage limits, conserve credits, catch bugs automatically, reduce manual approval overhead, and improve development speed.

agent_1.name: Claude Code
agent_1.interaction_model: Proposes changes, presents plan, offers approval modes (auto-accept, manual approve, keep planning)

agent_2.name: Antigravity Agent
agent_2.delegation: Delegates search to Gemini for live web retrieval

agent_3.name: Gemini
agent_3.purpose: Prevents Claude from hallucinating outdated implementations

actions: Search web for latest docs and libraries
Validate proposed implementation against current best practices
Loop back to planning if needed

benefits: Conserves credits by delegating search to Gemini
Catches bugs automatically via multi-agent review
target_audience: Developers using AI-assisted coding workflows who want to maximise output while minimising cost and errors
key_takeaway: By chaining three specialised agents — a builder (Claude), a search layer (Antigravity), and a context engine (Gemini) — you create a self-correcting development loop that is faster, cheaper, and more reliable than any single agent alone.
